title: Synthetic conformance fixtures + round-trip test
context: The validator (validate-001) and the FHIR IG (ig-001) both require a set of synthetic conformance fixtures — representative rare-cancer records that are provably synthetic and carry the required synthetic-data attestation, usable as both test inputs and examples. These fixtures must exercise the core CDE set, the crosswalk round-trips, and governance fields. Every fixture must pass the CI 'no real data' scan and the provenance linter. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-crosswalk-001.
objective: Generate a suite of synthetic conformance fixtures in `fixtures/` that: (1) represent realistic but entirely generated rare-cancer CDE records, (2) each carry a valid synthetic-data attestation per the gov-001 format, (3) are used as inputs for the crosswalk round-trip tests (CDE → mCODE FHIR, OMOP, Phenopackets → validate back), and (4) cover the minimal core plus at least one optional module and the consent-metadata module.
acceptanceCriteria: All fixtures are provably synthetic: generated (not sampled from real records), each carrying a valid synthetic-data attestation in the format defined by gov-001.
CI 'no real data' scan passes on every fixture; provenance linter passes.
At least one fixture exercises: the minimal-core CDE fields, the consent-metadata module, and one optional module (e.g., diagnosis/histology).
Fixtures are used as inputs for automated crosswalk round-trip tests (CDE → mCODE FHIR → validate; CDE → OMOP CDM → validate; CDE → Phenopackets → validate); tests pass.
`pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green.
Fixtures are committed to `fixtures/` with a CREDITS note confirming fully synthetic origin.
output: A `fixtures/` directory of synthetic conformance records with attestations and automated round-trip tests, committed via PR with green CI.
